title: Costa Rica 14-Day Nature Retreat
metaTitle: Costa Rica 14-Day Nature Retreat | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Costa Rica, Costa Rica: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Poás Volcano National Park and Arenal Volcano National Park. Prices from $1425 per person (estimate).
highlights: Zip-lining through the Monteverde cloud forest canopy
Soak in the Tabacón hot springs at sunset
Poás Volcano and La Paz Waterfall Gardens
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Costa Rica, Costa Rica — Tropical climate with a dry season (Dec-Apr) and a green/rainy season (May-Nov) bringing frequent but often short afternoon downpours; conditions vary significantly by elevation and coast. December to April, the dry season known locally as "verano," for the best wildlife viewing and beach weather. This itinerary blends Poás Volcano National Park, Arenal Volcano National Park, Mercado Central San José with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Poás Volcano National Park, Arenal Volcano National Park, Mercado Central San José
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Costa Rica
itinerary.description: Land at Juan Santamaría International Airport (SJO) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Poás Volcano National Park — Active volcano with a steaming crater lake. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Costa Rica: Arenal Volcano National Park & La Fortuna Waterfall
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Arenal Volcano National Park — Trails around Costa Rica's most iconic volcano. In the afternoon, continue to La Fortuna Waterfall — 70m waterfall with a swimmable pool. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Costa Rica: Manuel Antonio National Park & Monteverde Cloud Forest Reserve
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Manuel Antonio National Park — Rainforest-meets-beach park with abundant wildlife. In the afternoon, continue to Monteverde Cloud Forest Reserve — Misty cloud forest with hanging bridges. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Poás Volcano and La Paz Waterfall Gardens
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Poás Volcano and La Paz Waterfall Gardens, a popular side trip from Costa Rica. Return to Costa Rica in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Poás Volcano and La Paz Waterfall Gardens

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Rio Celeste turquoise waterfall — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Costa Rica: Tabacón Hot Springs & Sky Adventures Arenal
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Tabacón Hot Springs — Volcanically heated thermal river resort. In the afternoon, continue to Sky Adventures Arenal — Gondola, hanging bridges, and zipline above the rainforest. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Arenal Volcano and La Fortuna
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Arenal Volcano and La Fortuna, a popular side trip from Costa Rica. Return to Costa Rica in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Arenal Volcano and La Fortuna

itinerary.title: Costa Rica: Tortuguero National Park boat tour & Doka Estate coffee tour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Tortuguero National Park boat tour — Canal wildlife-spotting boat safari. In the afternoon, continue to Doka Estate coffee tour — Working coffee plantation tour and tasting. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Corcovado National Park (Osa Peninsula) — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Monteverde Cloud Forest
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Monteverde Cloud Forest, a popular side trip from Costa Rica. Return to Costa Rica in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Monteverde Cloud Forest

itinerary.title: Costa Rica: Rainforest Adventures Braulio Carrillo aerial tram & Mercado Central San José
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Rainforest Adventures Braulio Carrillo aerial tram — Gondola ride through the rainforest canopy. In the afternoon, continue to Mercado Central San José — Historic central market for food and crafts. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Rainforest Adventures Braulio Carrillo aerial tram

itinerary.title: Costa Rica: Plaza de la Cultura & Barrio Escalante
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Plaza de la Cultura — Central square beside the Gold Museum and National Theatre. In the afternoon, continue to Barrio Escalante — Trendy restaurant and craft-beer district. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Nauyaca Waterfalls — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Avenida Central or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to SJO for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book domestic flights or shuttles between regions in advance, as driving distances take longer than they look
Visit volcano and cloud forest parks early morning for the best wildlife activity and clearer skies
Keep small colones or USD cash for rural areas that don't take cards
Respect wildlife distance guidelines — never feed monkeys or approach sloths
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
A reliable rain jacket for the green season
Insect repellent for rainforest and lowland areas
Water shoes for waterfalls and river activities
thingsToDo: Rainforest Adventures Braulio Carrillo aerial tram
Zip-lining through the Monteverde cloud forest canopy
White-water rafting on the Pacuare River
Volcano hiking at Arenal or Poás
Surfing at Tamarindo or Santa Teresa
thingsToAvoid: Swimming at beaches without lifeguards or with unfamiliar rip currents
Leaving valuables in rental cars at trailhead parking lots
Feeding or approaching wildlife like monkeys and raccoons
Underestimating travel times on winding mountain roads
topRestaurants: Soda Tapia — Traditional casado (Costa Rican set meal) ($)
Restaurante Silvestre — Fine dining Costa Rican ($$$)
Kalú Café & Food Shop — Costa Rican-international fusion ($$)
Restaurante Nuestra Tierra — Traditional Tico cuisine ($$)
Café de los Deseos — Costa Rican cafe fare ($$)
shoppingAreas: Feria Verde de Aranjuez organic market
instagramSpots: Arenal Volcano with La Fortuna Waterfall
Monteverde hanging bridges in the cloud forest
familyActivities: Sky Adventures Arenal gondola and zipline
La Paz Waterfall Gardens and wildlife sanctuary
Manuel Antonio National Park wildlife walk
adventureActivities: Zip-lining through the Monteverde cloud forest canopy
White-water rafting on the Pacuare River
Volcano hiking at Arenal or Poás
Surfing at Tamarindo or Santa Teresa
luxuryExperiences: Private eco-lodge stay in the rainforest canopy
Hot springs resort retreat at Tabacón
Private wildlife-spotting boat tour in Tortuguero
honeymoonExperiences: Soak in the Tabacón hot springs at sunset
Private beach dinner in Manuel Antonio
Secluded eco-lodge stay with rainforest views
budgetExperiences: Free hiking trails in outer sections of many national parks
Lunch at a soda (casual eatery) at Mercado Central
Public beach access at Manuel Antonio or Jacó
Self-guided wildlife spotting along roadside trails
localEtiquette: Greet with a handshake; "Pura Vida" is both a greeting and a laid-back way of life.
A 10% service charge is often already included in restaurant bills.
Dress casually but modestly away from beach areas.
Maintain distance from wildlife and never feed animals in national parks.
Punctuality is relaxed ("Tico time"), especially outside business settings.
localTransportGuide: Domestic flights (Sansa, Green Airways) are the fastest way to cover Costa Rica's varied regions; shared shuttle vans like Interbus connect major tourist towns affordably. Renting a 4x4 offers flexibility, though mountain and rural roads can be rough, especially in the rainy season.
bestTimeToVisit: December to April, the dry season known locally as "verano," for the best wildlife viewing and beach weather.
weather: Tropical climate with a dry season (Dec-Apr) and a green/rainy season (May-Nov) bringing frequent but often short afternoon downpours; conditions vary significantly by elevation and coast.
visaInformation: Visa-free tourist entry for up to 90 days for most Western nationalities (US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia); a valid passport and onward ticket are required, with no advance e-visa needed for most tourists.
averageFlightTime: ~16.9h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Costa Rica?
faqs.answer: Visa-free tourist entry for up to 90 days for most Western nationalities (US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia); a valid passport and onward ticket are required, with no advance e-visa needed for most tourists.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Costa Rica?
faqs.answer: December to April, the dry season known locally as "verano," for the best wildlife viewing and beach weather. Tropical climate with a dry season (Dec-Apr) and a green/rainy season (May-Nov) bringing frequent but often short afternoon downpours; conditions vary significantly by elevation and coast.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Costa Rica safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Costa Rica is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Costa Rica?
faqs.answer: The local currency is CRC. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Costa Rica is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Soak in the Tabacón hot springs at sunset experience. It also works well for families — highlights include Sky Adventures Arenal gondola and zipline.

reviews.author: Tom Andersen
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Mercado Central San José and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Isabella Silva
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Tortuguero National Park boat tour and Avenida Central. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Daniel Patel
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Tabacón Hot Springs and Parque Metropolitano La Sabana. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Sofia Müller
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: Booked this as a group trip and it was well paced. La Fortuna Waterfall in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Omar Rossi
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Mercado Central San José and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. Rainforest Adventures Braulio Carrillo aerial tram in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Wei Brown
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Arenal Volcano National Park in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Nadia Cohen
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Costa Rica is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Barrio Escalante. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.
